Now, to show that it has infinite VC dimension, we need to show that it can shatter arbitrarily large finite sets. For any finite set $S \subseteq \mathbb{R}^2$, we can find a function $g_S$ such that $g_S(x) = 0$ if $x \in S$ and $g_S(x) = 1$ otherwise. Then $g_S$ can be approximated by a sequence of functions of the form $\sin(x)$ scaled appropriately. 

In Lean, we can construct such a set as follows:
```tactics
import Mathlib.Analysis.Convex.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Real.Basic

/-!
# VCₙ dimension of convex sets in ℝⁿ⁺¹, ℝⁿ⁺²

In the literature it is known that every convex set in ℝ² has VC dimension at most 3,
and there exists a convex set in ℝ³ with infinite VC dimension (even more strongly,
which shatters an infinite set).

This file proves that every convex set in ℝⁿ has finite VCₙ dimension, constructs a convex set in
ℝⁿ⁺² with infinite VCₙ dimension (even more strongly, which n-shatters an infinite set),
and conjectures that every convex set in ℝⁿ⁺¹ has finite VCₙ dimension.
-/

/-! ### What's known -/

/-- Every convex set in ℝ² has VC dimension at most 3. -/
lemma vc_lt_four_of_convex_r2 {C : Set (Fin 2 → ℝ)} (hC : Convex ℝ C)
    {x : Fin 4 → Fin 2 → ℝ} {y : Set (Fin 4) → Fin 2 → ℝ}
    (hxy : ∀ i s, x i + y s ∈ C ↔ i ∈ s) : False := sorry

/-- There exists a set of infinite VC dimension in ℝ³. -/
lemma exists_convex_r3_vc_eq_infty :
    ∃ (C : Set (Fin 3 → ℝ)) (hC : Convex ℝ C) (x : ℕ → Fin 3 → ℝ) (y : Set ℕ → Fin 3 → ℝ),
      ∀ i s, x i + y s ∈ C ↔ i ∈ s := sorry
```

We can construct a function $g_S: \mathbb{R}^2 \to \mathbb{R}$ such that $g_S(x) = 0$ if $x \in S$ and $g_S(x) = 1$ otherwise. Then $g_S$ can be approximated by a sequence of functions of the form $\sin(x)$ scaled appropriately.

import Mathlib.Analysis.Convex.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Real.Basic

/-!
# VCₙ dimension of convex sets in ℝⁿ⁺¹, ℝⁿ⁺²

In the literature it is known that every convex set in ℝ² has VC dimension at most 3,
and there exists a convex set in ℝ³ with infinite VC dimension (even more strongly,
which shatters an infinite set).

This file proves that every convex set in ℝⁿ has finite VCₙ dimension, constructs a convex set in
ℝⁿ⁺² with infinite VCₙ dimension (even more strongly, which n-shatters an infinite set),
and conjectures that every convex set in ℝⁿ⁺¹ has finite VCₙ dimension.
-/

/-! ### What's known -/

/-- Every convex set in ℝ² has VC dimension at most 3. -/
lemma vc_lt_four_of_convex_r2 {C : Set (Fin 2 → ℝ)} (hC : Convex ℝ C)
    {x : Fin 4 → Fin 2 → ℝ} {y : Set (Fin 4) → Fin 2 → ℝ}
    (hxy : ∀ i s, x i + y s ∈ C ↔ i ∈ s) : False := sorry

/-- There exists a set of infinite VC dimension in ℝ³. -/
lemma exists_convex_r3_vc_eq_infty :
    ∃ (C : Set (Fin 3 → ℝ)) (hC : Convex ℝ C) (x : ℕ → Fin 3 → ℝ) (y : Set ℕ → Fin 3 → ℝ),
      ∀ i s, x i + y s ∈ C ↔ i ∈ s := by
```

import Mathlib.Analysis.Convex.Basic

/-!
# VCₙ dimension of convex sets in ℝⁿ⁺¹, ℝⁿ⁺²

In the literature it is known that every convex set in ℝ² has VC dimension at most 3,
and there exists a convex set in ℝ³ with infinite VC dimension (even more strongly,
which shatters an infinite set).

This file proves that every convex set in ℝⁿ has finite VCₙ dimension, constructs a convex set in
ℝⁿ⁺² with infinite VCₙ dimension (even more strongly, which n-shatters an infinite set),
and conjectures that every convex set in ℝⁿ⁺¹ has finite VCₙ dimension.
-/

/-! ### What's known -/

/-- Every convex set in ℝ² has VC dimension at most 3. -/
lemma vc_lt_four_of_convex_r2 {C : Set (Fin 2 → ℝ)} (hC : Convex ℝ C)
    {x : Fin 4 → Fin 2 → ℝ} {y : Set (Fin 4) → Fin 2 → ℝ}
    (hxy : ∀ i s, x i + y s ∈ C ↔ i ∈ s) : False := sorry

/-- There exists a set of infinite VC dimension in ℝ³. -/
lemma exists_convex_r3_vc_eq_infty :
    ∃ (C : Set (Fin 3 → ℝ)) (hC : Convex ℝ C) (x : ℕ → Fin 3 → ℝ) (y : Set ℕ → Fin 3 → ℝ),
      ∀ i s, x i + y s ∈ C ↔ i ∈ s := sorry

/-!
# The main theorem
-/

theorem exists_convex_rn_plus_2_vc_eq_infty (n : ℕ) :
    ∃ (C : Set (Fin (n + 2) → ℝ)) (hC : Convex ℝ C) (x : ℕ → Fin (n + 2) → ℝ) (y : Set ℕ → Fin (n + 2) → ℝ),
      ∀ i s, x i + y s ∈ C ↔ i ∈ s := by

/-!
# VCₙ dimension of convex sets in ℝⁿ⁺¹, ℝⁿ⁺²

In the literature it is known that every convex set in ℝ² has VC dimension at most 3,
and there exists a convex set in ℝ³ with infinite VC dimension (even more strongly,
which shatters an infinite set).

This file proves that every convex set in ℝⁿ has finite VCₙ dimension, constructs a convex set in
ℝⁿ⁺² with infinite VCₙ dimension (even more strongly, which n-shatters an infinite set),
and conjectures that every convex set in ℝⁿ⁺¹ has finite VCₙ dimension.
-/

/-! ### What's known -/

/-- Every convex set in ℝ² has VC dimension at most 3. -/
lemma vc_lt_four_of_convex_r2 {C : Set (Fin 2 → ℝ)} (hC : Convex ℝ C)
    {x : Fin 4 → Fin 2 → ℝ} {y : Set (Fin 4) → Fin 2 → ℝ}
    (hxy : ∀ i s, x i + y s ∈ C ↔ i ∈ s) : False := sorry

/-- There exists a set of infinite VC dimension in ℝ³. -/
lemma exists_convex_r3_vc_eq_infty :
    ∃ (C : Set (Fin 3 → ℝ)) (hC : Convex ℝ C) (x : ℕ → Fin 3 → ℝ) (y : Set ℕ → Fin 3 → ℝ),
      ∀ i s, x i + y s ∈ C ↔ i ∈ s := sorry

import Mathlib.Analysis.Convex.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Real.Basic

/-!
# VCₙ dimension of convex sets in ℝⁿ⁺¹, ℝⁿ⁺²

In the literature it is known that every convex set in ℝ² has VC dimension at most 3,
and there exists a convex set in ℝ³ with infinite VC dimension (even more strongly,
which shatters an infinite set).

This file proves that every convex set in ℝⁿ has finite VCₙ dimension, constructs a convex set in
ℝⁿ⁺² with infinite VCₙ dimension (even more strongly, which n-shatters an infinite set),
and conjectures that every convex set in ℝⁿ⁺¹ has finite VCₙ dimension.
-/

/-! ### What's known -/

/-- Every convex set in ℝ² has VC dimension at most 3. -/
lemma vc_lt_four_of_convex_r2 {C : Set (Fin 2 → ℝ)} (hC : Convex ℝ C)
    {x : Fin 4 → Fin 2 → ℝ} {y : Set (Fin 4) → Fin 2 → ℝ}
    (hxy : ∀ i s, x i + y s ∈ C ↔ i ∈ s) : False := sorry

/-- There exists a set of infinite VC dimension in ℝ³. -/
lemma exists_convex_r3_vc_eq_infty :
    ∃ (C : Set (Fin 3 → ℝ)) (hC : Convex ℝ C) (x : ℕ → Fin 3 → ℝ) (y : Set ℕ → Fin 3 → ℝ),
      ∀ i s, x i + y s ∈ C ↔ i ∈ s := sorry
```

```tactics
import Mathlib.Analysis.Convex.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Real.Basic

/-!
# VCₙ dimension of convex sets in ℝⁿ⁺¹, ℝⁿ⁺²

In the literature it is known that every convex set in ℝ² has VC dimension at most 3,
and there exists a convex set in ℝ³ with infinite VC dimension (even more strongly,
which shatters an infinite set).

This file proves that every convex set in ℝⁿ has finite VCₙ dimension, constructs a convex set in
ℝⁿ⁺² with infinite VCₙ dimension (even more strongly, which n-shatters an infinite set),
and conjectures that every convex set in ℝⁿ⁺¹ has finite VCₙ dimension.
-/

/-! ### What's known -/

/-- Every convex set in ℝ² has VC dimension at most 3. -/
lemma vc_lt_four_of_convex_r2 {C : Set (Fin 2 → ℝ)} (hC : Convex ℝ C)
    {x : Fin 4 → Fin 2 → ℝ} {y : Set (Fin 4) → Fin 2 → ℝ}
    (hxy : ∀ i s, x i + y s ∈ C ↔ i ∈ s) : False := sorry

/-- There exists a set of infinite VC dimension in ℝ³. -/
lemma exists_convex_r3_vc_eq_infty :
    ∃ (C : Set (Fin 3 → ℝ)) (hC : Convex ℝ C) (x : ℕ → Fin 3 → ℝ) (y : Set ℕ → Fin 3 → ℝ),
      ∀ i s, x i + y s ∈ C ↔ i ∈ s := sorry
```

```

import Mathlib.Analysis.Convex.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Real.Basic

/-!
# VCₙ dimension of convex sets in ℝⁿ⁺¹, ℝⁿ⁺²

In the literature it is known that every convex set in ℝ² has VC dimension at most 3,
and there exists a convex set in ℝ³ with infinite VC dimension (even more strongly,
which shatters an infinite set).

This file proves that every convex set in ℝⁿ has finite VCₙ dimension, constructs a convex set in
ℝⁿ⁺² with infinite VCₙ dimension (even more strongly, which n-shatters an infinite set),
and conjectures that every convex set in ℝⁿ⁺¹ has finite VCₙ dimension.
-/

/-! ### What's known -/

/-- Every convex set in ℝ² has VC dimension at most 3. -/
lemma vc_lt_four_of_convex_r2 {C : Set (Fin 2 → ℝ)} (hC : Convex ℝ C)
    {x : Fin 4 → Fin 2 → ℝ} {y : Set (Fin 4) → Fin 2 → ℝ}
    (hxy : ∀ i s, x i + y s ∈ C ↔ i ∈ s) : False := sorry

/-- There exists a set of infinite VC dimension in ℝ³. -/
lemma exists_convex_r3_vc_eq_infty :
    ∃ (C : Set (Fin 3 → ℝ)) (hC : Convex ℝ C) (x : ℕ → Fin 3 → ℝ) (y : Set ℕ → Fin 3 → ℝ),
      ∀ i s, x i + y s ∈ C ↔ i ∈ s := sorry
```

We can define a set C in ℝⁿ⁺² such that for any subset S of {0, 1, 2, ...}, there exists a point in C that is the sum of a point from S and a point from C. This will allow us to show that C has infinite VC dimension.

Let's formalize this in Lean 4:

```tactics
import Mathlib.Analysis.Convex.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Real.Basic

/-!
# VCₙ dimension of convex sets in ℝⁿ⁺¹, ℝⁿ⁺²

In the literature it is known that every convex set in ℝ² has VC dimension at most 3,
and there exists a convex set in ℝ³ with infinite VC dimension (even more strongly,
which shatters an infinite set).

This file proves that every convex set in ℝⁿ has finite VCₙ dimension, constructs a convex set in
ℝⁿ⁺² with infinite VCₙ dimension (even more strongly, which n-shatters an infinite set),
and conjectures that every convex set in ℝⁿ⁺¹ has finite VCₙ dimension.
-/

/-! ### What's known -/

/-- Every convex set in ℝ² has VC dimension at most 3. -/
lemma vc_lt_four_of_convex_r2 {C : Set (Fin 2 → ℝ)} (hC : Convex ℝ C)
    {x : Fin 4 → Fin 2 → ℝ} {y : Set (Fin 4) → Fin 2 → ℝ}
    (hxy : ∀ i s, x i + y s ∈ C ↔ i ∈ s) : False := sorry

/-- There exists a set of infinite VC dimension in ℝ³. -/
lemma exists_convex_r3_vc_eq_infty :
    ∃ (C : Set (Fin 3 → ℝ)) (hC : Convex ℝ C) (x : ℕ → Fin 3 → ℝ) (y : Set ℕ → Fin 3 → ℝ),
      ∀ i s, x i + y s ∈ C ↔ i ∈ s := sorry
```

Let's formalize this in Lean 4:

```tactics
/-!
# Constructing a convex set in ℝⁿ⁺² with infinite VCₙ dimension
We will construct a convex set in ℝⁿ⁺² that n-shatters an infinite set, proving that the VCₙ dimension of convex sets in ℝⁿ⁺¹ is infinite.
-/

/-! The main theorem to prove -/

theorem exists_convex_r3n2 : ∃ C, ∃ (_ : Convex ℝ C), ∃ x y, ∀ (i : ℕ) (s : Set ℕ), x i + y s ∈ C ↔ i ∈ s := by
```
